Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers! At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. 

Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights. 

We are looking for Construction Technicians to be based in and around Adelaide, South Australia.

About you 

You will be the face of the OTIS brand and responsible for installing lifts, escalators, and travelators in compliance with the relevant codes and Otis specifications and procedures. 

This permanent role will be reporting to the Project Manager and working closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters & Field Engineers as well as Construction Coordinators. 

Permanent role

Reports to the Construction Project Manager

Key Responsibilities 

Install and Modernise lifts, escalators, and travelators and work to the highest safety and environmental standards while meeting service targets. 

Ensure timely completion of defects identified during service / handing over inspection in coordination with the Project Manager. 

Occasional travel to regional Adelaide.

Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers of your processes. 

Maintain company tools, equipment, and materials properly and safeguard such property. 

Perform work in accordance with the relevant lift industry codes and standards and maintain and work to the Quality System. 

Comply with the company's Environmental Health and Safety policies and procedures, including reporting any conditions that may impact on the environment or any employee. 

What you will need to be successful 

Have a Electrical trade qualification or equivalent.

Min 5 years work experience as a Construction Technician.

Have Working at Heights, Rigging and Dogging experience.

Hold a valid driver's license

Be available for occasional travel to regional Adelaide and able to work unsupervised. 

Enjoys communicating with various stakeholders at different levels and working closely with others in the field. 

Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training. 

Able to plan and prioritize tasks and complete them in a timely manner. 

Key Benefits

Industry-leading compensation and benefits. 

Be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers.

Meaningful career opportunities for people to grow through excellent Learning programs.

Becoming part of the largest vertical transportation company in the world and gaining exposure to the benefits and opportunities offered.

About us

We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.

We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.

Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion

We “lift” our people to their highest potential.

Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.

We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality, and inclusion. We strive to be a best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders, sexual orientation, races, and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And a place where every voice feels safe, welcomed and heard.

We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career. This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.

Otis is An Equal Opportunity, Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age, or any other protected class according to applicable law. Otis are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive place to work. We strongly encourage applications from women, and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people

For more information: https://www.otis.com/en/au/our-company/diversity-and-inclusion

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our dynamic team at BOSS Elevators. In this role, you will play a crucial part in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sunshine Coast to Discovery Coast region.

As an expert technician, you’ll be responsible for conducting routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs, with a focus on quickly diagnosing and resolving issues with a wide range of elevator components. Your ability to think critically and implement effective solutions will be essential to ensuring optimal functionality and compliance with AS1735 standards.

You will liaise directly with clients to understand their concerns, provide clear communication, and deliver exceptional service. You will have the opportunity to work both independently and as part of a skilled team committed to "Leading the Change" in elevator maintenance.

This role is suited for someone who is not only technically skilled but also values quality workmanship, safety, and client satisfaction. If you take pride in overcoming challenges and delivering high-quality results, this could be the perfect opportunity for you. 

What you'll be doing

Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing

Diagnose & Troubleshoot Issues: Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as motors, controllers, and safety devices. Your expertise will be pivotal in delivering swift, accurate solutions

Carry Out Repair Work: Conduct precise repairs, replacing worn or faulty parts as needed to restore full elevator functionality and reliability.

Maintain Detailed Records: Keep thorough, accurate records of all maintenance, service, and repair activities, ensuring clear documentation for compliance and future reference.

Ensure Safety & Compliance: Maintain strict adherence to all safety standards and regulations, upholding safety as a non-negotiable priority in every task.

Provide On-Call Support: Be prepared to respond to emergency situations as part of our rotational standby roster, ensuring timely support and effective resolution of urgent issues.

Liaise with Clients: Engage professionally with clients to understand their requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that reinforce our commitment to superior service.

Continuous Skill Development: Participate in training events and workshops. 

What we're looking for

Positive, proactive attitude and a commitment to safety and quality service.

Minimum 5 years of experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.

Queensland Electrical Licence (no restrictions).

White card.

Working at Heights certification (favourable but not essential).

Rigging and Dogging certification (favourable but not essential).

Certificate IV in WHS (favourable but not essential).

Must have the right to work in Australia. Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.

What we offer

Competitive Remuneration: Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications. 

Coastal Lifestyle: Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most sought-after coastal regions.

Motivating Team Culture: We believe in creating a positive work environment where your skills are valued, and your career can thrive.

Vehicle and Tools: The whole kit and caboodle 

Application Process

Please submit your resume, outlining your experience, qualifications, and referees, by 15 March 2026

Applications must also include a cover letter explaining why you are an ideal candidate for this role.

Shortlisted applicants will be contacted by 15 March 2026.

Interviews will be completed by 20 March 2026, with an employment offer extended to the successful candidate shortly thereafter.

jobs.description:
WHY join Schindler?

At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their full potential through careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our business in the coming years

At Schindler, We Elevate...

As a leading employer in the urban mobility industry, we look for highly engaging professionals who are driven to grow and advance their careers at a first-class organisation. Our goal is to have a highly motivated and engaged workforce led by talented management in a work environment that is stimulating and attractive to both current and prospective employees.

About the role:

As our Administrator, you will be responsible for timesheet management, rosters, ordering PPE, handling customer enquiries, and supporting field staff with a range of tasks, including technological issues and a vast range of other administrative duties. The role is a fast-paced, highly reactive role that reports to an experienced Branch Support Lead and will see you working closely with our team of skilled administrators. Our Melbourne team is based in Port Melbourne. You will liaise with key stakeholders and customers to ensure a high level of customer service is provided.

Previous experience working as a Service Coordinator, Service Administrator, Branch Administrator or similar in an industry such as Lift, HVAC, Fire, Security, Electrical, Mechanical or Plumbing will be highly regarded

What’s in it for you:

$72K base + 12% Super
5% annual Bonus
Excellent opportunity for you to expand your experience at world-leading Lift company
Permanent full-time role
A work/life balance organisation - culture-first approach
A great learning environment with a local and global team
Access to the Employee Benefits Discount Scheme
A wide variety of social activities – even a day off for your birthday!
Fitness passport with access to over 1,700 fitness facilities across Australia
Office Based in - Port Melbourne

Key Accountabilities:

Ensuring all work orders are captured in the system
Organising customer inductions and medicals for field staff
Updating rosters
Handling customer enquiries and escalating when required
Raising purchase requests for uniforms, stationary, parts, labour etc.
Providing customers with periodic performance reports
Maintaining and reviewing timesheets for payroll
Customer invoicing
Adhoc administrative tasks

What we are looking for:

Previous experience in an administration role, preferably within a service-based industry
Demonstrated customer service or service coordinator experience
Strong attention to detail and an ability to multitask multiple priorities
Intermediate MS office skills
Experience with SAP or similar ERP systems
Good written and verbal communication skills and the ability to work in a team
A desire for growth and to expand your knowledge and skillset

About Schindler:

Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

HOW to apply:

We are constantly looking for passionate and enthusiastic people with the energy and drive to build relationships and networks. At Schindler, you will find a highly motivated and engaged working environment that lets you grow as an individual and allows you to plan your career.

If you are seeking an opportunity to join a Global Industry Leader and believe that you possess the required skills and qualifications to succeed in this role, click on the "" button

If you are an existing Schindler employee, please ensure you have discussed your application with your line manager prior to applying.

● AUD 85,000 to 115,00 per year + superannuation

● Location of Employment: Caringbah, Sydney, NSW 2229

About Us:

Old Mate Elevators (OME) is an established company in Sydney that has been in operation for more than 25 years. We provide a highly supportive environment for all our employees.

The successful applicant MUST be a motivated and self-driven individual who is seeking a long-term, full-time position. You will be working with a team that has over 60 years of combined experience and knowledge that you will be able to leverage information and learn from.

The Role

We are seeking a talented and passionate Elevator Fitter to join our team. In this role, you will be working in conjunction with the Installation Project Manager and Service Coordinator and reporting to the Operations Manager to provide best practice in the installation of lift components, maintenance, servicing, and troubleshooting of all vertical transport products and related equipment supplied or maintained by Old Mate Elevators Pty Ltd (OME). Working with the Installation Project Manager and Service Coordinator to streamline and improve efficiencies within the departments and meet KPIs on a quarterly basis.

Responsibilities:

The Elevator Fitter will be responsible for the following tasks:

· Reviewing elevator blueprints, wiring diagrams, and technical specifications to determine project requirements, identify necessary materials, and plan the sequence of elevator installation, wiring, and operational testing.

· Devise, implement, and maintain best practice installation solutions.

· Fitting and securing manufactured metal components and subassemblies, such as guide rails, car frames, counterweights, and machinery, within lift shafts and machine rooms.

· Verifying the accuracy, clearance, and proper fit of installed mechanical and structural parts using precision measuring tools such as vernier calipers, micrometers, dial indicators, feeler gauges, laser alignment tools, and spirit levels to ensure compliance with strict safety and operational standards.

· Ensure project scope is adhered to throughout the installation process.

· Adjusting elevator guide rails, stops, and control systems during installation to ensure proper alignment and smooth operation. Installing and configuring specialized tools and equipment required for on-site modifications to elevator components.

· Precisely forming metal components and assemblies using techniques like cutting, grinding, and drilling to achieve required tolerances for proper alignment and integration within the lift system.

· Cutting, threading, bending, and installing hydraulic and pneumatic pipes and lines that power lift doors, braking systems, and other auxiliary functions.

· Setting up and adjusting the mechanical and electromechanical components that govern the operation and safety features of the lift.

· Diagnosing mechanical and hydraulic faults in installed lifts, performing routine operational maintenance, and carrying out overhauls and repairs on mechanical parts and fluid power equipment.

· Rectify breakdown issues.

· Procure the required materials to resolve the breakdown.

· Work in a timely and conscientious manner to carry out extensive checks of all critical safety apparatus on the vertical transport product, including emergency telephone systems.

· Assembling and installing complete lift systems on-site in both residential and commercial buildings, ensuring all mechanical components are correctly integrated and ready for electrical connection and testing.

· Ensure that operational H&S requirements as per Old Mate Elevators (OME) H&S Policy & Procedure document are met and adhered to.

Promptly report all accidents, hazards, near-miss events, and property damage by advising the office at your first opportunity.

Skills and Qualifications Required:

Successful applicants must have:

● Certificate III in Engineering - Mechanical Trade, which covers fitting, machining, maintenance, and repair, Certificate IV in Engineering, or its equivalent. However, at least three years of relevant experience may substitute for the formal qualifications.

● Vertical Transport industry knowledge

● AS1735 Lift knowledge

● White Card

● Ability to work as part of a team to share knowledge, especially in the up-skilling of apprentices.

● Ability to work independently (self-motivated) to achieve company targets.

● High level of initiative.

● Ability to work under pressure and achieve deadlines.

● Strong customer service focus.

● Personal integrity, well organised.

● Confident and articulate.

Ready to take the next step in your career? Submit your application today and become part of a company that values creativity, technical expertise, and professional growth.

We encourage all qualified applicants to apply and are committed to providing equal opportunity to everyone. However, to comply with relevant regulations, preference will be given to Australian citizens and permanent residents.

jobs.title: Inclusion and diversity
jobs.description:
Home
Responsibility
People
Inclusion & Diversity
Inclusion & Diversity
"We are all in!"

Our global presence gives us access to the full spectrum of human diversity – reinforcing our ability to adapt and innovate.

We believe that where we work – both physical and virtual – must be inclusive in all aspects. It must be a place where we welcome people of all backgrounds and empower them to achieve great things - a place where we practice equity.


We appreciate the full scope of human differences, including ethnic background, gender, culture, religion, nationality, age, disability or health status, race, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, as well as personality, preferences and working styles. All of this, and more, sway how we see each other as similar or different.

And we like different. After all, Inclusive and diverse teams drive more innovative ideas and thinking, which is good for all of us at Schindler and our customers.


Inclusion is key to create an environment that welcomes all aspects of diversity and ensures the organization benefits from each person’s full contribution and unique perspective.

Wensi Wang, Head Global Inclusion & Diversity

Our Inclusion and Diversity Commitment

We commit to providing an inclusive work environment at Schindler, where diverse people feel welcomed to come together as they are to do their best work and to serve the over 2 billion people using our elevators, escalators, and moving walks every day. 

Download our complete commitment statement (PDF, 2 MB) ➝

Gender

Gender matters. We are committed to the UN Women's Empowerment Principles and feel strongly that our industry and our organization should reflect the mix of genders in society. Beyond our values, we know that mixed gender teams collaborate better and make better decisions. Through targeted development programs, training on unconscious bias, we’re working hard to build a mix of genders across Schindler. Our CEO reviews our progress regularly to make sure we stay on track. Also, our global equal pay initiative ensures that everyone receives fair pay for their role, no matter what their gender.

Over 300 women have taken part in leadership & advancement programs.
Diverse abilities

Mobility solutions are our business, and our technicians think inclusively every day when installing elevators, escalators, or moving walks. Abilities come in many different packages so, through initiatives like our Valuable 500 commitment, we are working to improve how we recruit and include people with disabilities. Several local programs have already helped increase the number of people with disabilities in our workforce.

Generations

We celebrate that there are up to five generations working together at Schindler, offering fantastic opportunities to learn from each other. We encourage every generation to remain open, curious, and ready to learn from others.

Sexual orientation & Gender identity

We know that our personal lives intersect more and more with our work lives. All of us need to feel safe at work to be able to be our true selves. Through training, we encourage all leaders and employees to step up and act as allies when something isn’t safe, or if someone is not treated with respect. We support the UN Standards of Conduct for Business Tackling Discrimination Against LGBTI People.

Schindler Switzerland proud to have been awarded the Swiss LGBTI* label
Ethnicity and Culture

We do not tolerate discrimination and are proud to do our part to fight biases to help make the world more just for all ethnicities. It is only natural that, as a global organization, we reflect the ethnic diversity found in the places where we operate. One way we do this is to promote international mobility, helping to build teams with diverse nationalities around the world.  

Employee inclusion networks

We actively work with our inclusion networks for gender, LGBTQIA+, multicultural /ethnicity, generations, diverse abilities and mental wellbeing, and U.S. veterans to shape our culture so that all colleagues feel part of the team and have a work environment where they feel they belong.

Equal Pay

We are committed to embracing the full scope of human diversity and fostering an inclusive working environment. We work strategically to eliminate biases and inequalities in order to ensure equal opportunities and equal access for all. Equal Pay is part of our inclusion and diversity strategy, and we ensure equal pay for equal work.

Marina S. and Mario F. from the Schindler team
Our Diversity at a glance
Over 69,000 employees
Over 130 nationalities
Over 1,000 branch offices in over 100 countries
Gender distribution of employees :
At the close of 2023, 13% female and 87% male*
 

*Even though globally databases are binary by government-requirement, we recognize that gender is not binary.

How we’re creating more inclusion
Together, as leaders and colleagues, we create an inclusive work environment that enables everyone to be their authentic selves at work. We want to make sure that everyone feels free to speak up and contribute to their fullest. 
Find out more about what we're doing
What we can do
Collectively, at Schindler we can all make the world more accessible for everyone by applying an inclusive mindset when we create our mobility solutions.
Read more about our inclusive mindset
Why it matters for all of us
Our values tell us that fostering inclusion and diversity is the right thing to do.

Join Victoria’s Leading Independent Lift Company

Southern Lifts is seeking a qualified and experienced A-Grade Lift Technician to step directly into this role. This is a hands-on leadership position for a senior technician who can confidently manage installs, service work, commissioning and on-site teams across a variety of lift systems.

No juniors. No general electricians. This role is strictly for a proven, fully licensed Lift Technician ready to lead.

About Southern Lifts

With over 23 years in the business, Southern Lifts is one of Victoria’s most trusted names in vertical transport. We specialise in high-end residential and low rise commercial lift solutions using premium international & domestic systems.

We’re based in Braeside, with a professional, tight-knit crew focused on delivering great work and backing each other on every job.

The Role – A-Grade Lift Technician

You’ll lead the technical delivery of lift projects and service work, with responsibilities including:

Servicing, maintaining, and fault diagnostics (on-site and remote)

Occasionally installing and commissioning a wide range of lift systems (hydraulic, traction, screw, MRL, platform)

Supervising and training apprentices as well as trade assistants

Overseeing apprentice profiling and progress

Overseeing site safety, compliance and documentation

Communicating with homeowners, builders, project managers & relevant parties

Ensuring all work meets OH&S, Australian Standards, and lift code compliance

Leading by example and maintaining a high-performance culture within the team

Keeping detailed job data via our digital CRM system (Elevate)

Participating in the on-call support roster

You Must Have

Qualifications & Licences:

Current A-Grade Victorian Electrical Licence with 5 years of proven interaction with ESV

Construction White Card

Manual Driver’s Licence

Working with children check

Working at Heights (preferred)

Experience:

Minimum 5 years’ experience in the lift industry

Proven record in leading installations, commissioning, and complex fault finding

Be comfortable supervising others and running multiple jobs at once

Skills:

Advanced fault-finding (electrical and mechanical)

Strong understanding of wiring schematics, site plans, and lift drawings

Computer literacy

Excellent communication, leadership, and time management

What You’ll Get

$90,000-130,000 salary, depending on experience

Fully maintained company vehicle + tool allowance + super

Full-time role joining the team of Technical Supervisor with direct leadership responsibilities

Long-term, stable work with a respected and growing lift company

A supportive team and management structure that’s got your back

Today, our focus more than ever is on people. As a global, people-powered company, we put people – passengers, customers, and colleagues – at the center of everything we do. Lift Service Technician | Melbourne, VIC | Full-time position

About Us
Tesla Elevators is a proudly Australian-owned and operated independent lift company, providing service and maintenance across residential, commercial and industrial sites. Our growth has been built on exceptional customer service, a dedicated technical team, and a strong commitment to work-life balance. We’re serious about quality, safety and professionalism, and we value a supportive culture and enjoying what we do.

The Role
We are seeking an experienced Lift Service Technician to join our Melbourne-based Service Team. Reporting to the Maintenance Coordinator, you will take ownership of a scheduled maintenance run and be responsible for servicing, repairs and breakdown response across a range of lift systems including hydraulic, MRL and platform lifts. You will work on our own products as well as a variety of third-party equipment.

This is a hands-on role for someone who takes pride in clean, compliant work, communicates well with customers, and can fault-find confidently to reduce repeat callouts.

What We Offer

· Company vehicle

· iPhone

· On-call allowance (rostered on-call)

· 36-hour work week

· 26 RDOs per annum

· Competitive remuneration with overtime opportunities

· Training, support and a team that values your contribution

Key Responsibilities

· Plan and execute your workload effectively in coordination with the Maintenance Coordinator

· Perform scheduled maintenance, repairs, breakdown response and call-out visits with care and attention to detail

· Diagnose faults, troubleshoot equipment and complete inspections and diagnostic testing to ensure reliability and performance

· Complete all work to manufacturer specifications, Australian Standards and regulatory requirements

· Maintain strict compliance with WHS policies and maintain safe operating conditions at all times

· Maintain a safe, tidy and professional worksite, with strong care and respect in customers’ homes and workplaces

· Communicate professionally with customers, keeping them informed and providing practical guidance on lift operation and care

· Accurately document work completed, including maintenance logs, breakdown reports, repair reports and emails, in clear language that non-technical customers and internal staff can understand

· Monitor your calendar daily for schedule updates and upcoming work

· Contribute to continuous improvement by helping refine workflows and service processes

What We’re Looking For

Qualifications (essential)

· Australian Electrical A-Grade Licence

· Construction White Card

· Current manual Driver’s Licence

· Full working rights in Australia (citizen or permanent resident)

Experience and technical capability

· Minimum 5 years’ experience as a lift technician (service and repairs focused preferred)

· Strong electrical and mechanical fault-finding skills

· Experience with hydraulic and traction drive systems (MRL and hydraulic)

· Ability to read and interpret electrical schematics

· Confidence with PLCs, microprocessors and VF drives is highly regarded

Personal attributes

· Self-motivated, solution-focused and team-oriented

· Organised, reliable and professional with customers

· Takes pride in doing the job properly, including clear reporting and clean workmanship

· Flexible and willing to travel within our service area as required

Checks

· Prepared to provide a National Police Check and Working with Children Check

Ready to Join Us?
If you’re ready to elevate your career with Tesla Elevators, apply now. We’re looking for a technician who wants stability, great conditions, and a team that backs quality work.
